# MAIN ARTICLE Contents lists available at ScienceDirect # Microchemical Journal journal homepage: www.elsevier.com/locate/microc # Ti3C2MXenes with intrinsic peroxidase-like activity for label-free and colorimetric sensing of proteins Miaomiao Li a,1 , Xiuying Peng b,1 , Yujie Han a , Lifang Fan a , Zhiguang Liu a , Yujing Guo a,* - a Institute of Environmental Science, College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Shanxi University, Taiyuan030006, China b Department of Environmental and Safety Engineering, Taiyuan Institute of Technology, Taiyuan030008, China A R T I C L E I N F O Keywords: Ti3C2NSs Peroxidase-like activity ssDNA Label-free Colorimetric TB # 1. Introduction Natural enzymes as efficient biocatalysts can catalyze chemical reactions related to life and promote the metabolism of living things. They have the advantages of high catalytic efficiency and strong specificity. However, some shortcomings, such as high cost, lack of stability and sensitivity in harsh chemical environment, seriously restrict their application [1 -3]. As a substitute for natural enzymes, nanozymes have received great attention [4,5], since Xiyun Yan and coworkers firstly reported that Fe4O3nanoparticles possessed the enzyme-like activities in2007 [6]. Compared to natural enzymes, nanozymes are less expensive, more stable, easier to store, greater recycling and reuse [5,7], therefore they exhibit great potential in biosensing, immunoassay, cancer diagnosis and pollutant removal et al. [6,8]. Until now, different nanomaterials including metals [9 -11], transition metal oxide [12 -14], caron-based nanomaterials [15 -18] have successfully mimicked various natural enzymes, such as peroxidase, oxidase, catalase, and superoxide dismutase [19]. In addition to the enzyme-like activities, nanozymes also possess characteristics of nanomaterials. Thus, they are more conducive to chemical modification and suitable for the construction of biosensors. * Corresponding author. E-mail address: guoyj@sxu.edu.cn (Y. Guo). 1Miaomiao Li and Xiuying Peng contributed equally to this work. A B S T R A C T MXenes are attractive for various biosensors owning to their tunable versatile properties. Herein, Ti3C2nanosheets (Ti3C2NSs) were found to possess intrinsic peroxidase-like activity, and which could be enhanced by single-stranded DNA (ssDNA) adsorbed on the nanosheets. Hence, a simple label-free sensing strategy was developed for colorimetric detection of biomolecules, based on Ti3C2NSs as a peroxidase mimic enzyme and ssDNA aptamer as an enzymatic activity enhancement factor along with a recognition substance for the target. In the presence of target biomolecules, the aptamers were desorbed from Ti3C2NSs due to the specific binding of targets to aptamers, thus bringing about the decrease of catalytic activity. Thrombin (TB) as the model was determined by this constructed biosensor with a linear range of1.0 × 10GLYPH<0> 11to1.0 × 10GLYPH<0> 8M, and evaluated subsequently in the real blood samples with satisfactory results. This work highlights Ti3C2MXenes as a nanomaterial mimic enzyme (called nanozyme) for monitoring biomolecules by target-specific recognition strategy. Many nanozymes with peroxidase-like activity were applied as biosensing platforms for direct determination of H2O2 [20 -22], glucose [23,24] and so on, or indirect analysis of biomolecules combining with specific recognition elements (aptamer or antibody) [4,25]. Such as, Guo et al. [17] provided a colorimetric sensing strategy for recognition of single nucleotide polymorphisms based on the peroxidase-like activity of hemin-graphene hybrid material and its different affinities with ssDNA and dsDNA. Wang et al. [7] developed a label-free colorimetric sensor for detecting exosomes by means of the specific aptamers accelerating the peroxidase-like activity of C3N4nanosheets. Li et al. [26] modified Fe3O4nanoparticles with ATP-specific aptamers to regulate the enzyme-like catalysis activity and achieved the quantitative trace of ATP. All of the above strategies use ssDNA either as a regulator of enzyme-like catalytic activity or as a stabilizer of nanomaterials. It has been effectively proved that the regulation of enzyme-like activities by ssDNA for oligonucleotides at the nano-interface can promote the affinity between the enzyme and the substrate [27]. For example, the activity of ssDNA functionalized Fe3O4nanoparticles reached six times higher than that of bare Fe3O4nanoparticles [26]. The maximum reaction rate of the substrate oxidation catalyzed by ssDNA modified C3N4nanosheets was over four times faster than that by bare nanosheets [7]. M. Li et al. Scheme1. Illustration of the label-free colorimetric biosensing platform based on the peroxidase-like activity of Ti3C2NSs. Furthermore, DNA functionalization can not only improve the colloidal stability of nanoparticles, but also enhance the recognition of metal ions, small molecules and proteins [28]. MXenes, two-dimensional (2D) transition-metal carbides and carbonitrides materials, have rapidly emerged since2011when a new2D structure of Ti3C2MXenes was introduced by Gogotsi and co-workers [29]. As a novel family of2D layered nanomaterials, MXenes are denoted as Mn + 1Xn, where M represents transition metal, X means carbon or nitrogen and n = 1,2,3 [30 -33]. MXenes exhibit excellent properties such as extraordinary energy storage, large specific surface areas, low temperature thermoelectricity, good electronic transport properties and good biocompatibility [34]. Especially, abundant oxygen or hydroxyl terminated surface and complete metal atomic layers endow them with easy interaction with many biomolecules through hydrogen bonds, van der Waals forces, electrostatic interactions and coordination bonds. These characteristics make them suitable for constructing various biosensors [35]. For instance, Ti3C2NSs were applied in fluorescence sensors for selective detection of human papillomavirus DNA [30] and exosomes [35], respectively, based on the fluorescence quenching capability of ultrathin Ti3C2NSs and the interaction between Ti3C2NSs and ssDNA. Nevertheless, colorimetric biosensors based on the peroxidase activity of Ti3C2NSs have not been reported yet. Herein, Ti3C2NSs were found with intrinsic peroxidase activity. Negatively charged ssDNA can be readily adsorbed on the Ti3C2NSs and boost the enzymatic catalytic activity of Ti3C2NSs. Aptamers are singlestranded DNA or RNA oligonucleotides which have high affinity and the specificity to their targets. They are ideal recognition elements for biosensing applications. Inspired by these findings, a label-free colorimetric biosensing platform was constructed based on the enzyme-like activity of Ti3C2NSs combined with the specific target recognition of aptamer. The sensing strategy is illustrated in Scheme1. The ssDNA aptamers are modified on the surface of Ti3C2NSs to form Ti3C2@ssDNA nanocomplex. This nanocomplex shows a significantly enhanced catalytic activity for the substrate o-phenylenediamine (OPD) oxidation reaction by H2O2. When the target molecules are introduced, the specific binding with the aptamers impels the aptamers desorbed from Ti3C2NSs, resulting in the decrease of enzymatic activity. The higher the concentration of the target, the weaker the catalytic activity of Ti3C2NSs towards the substrate oxidation, thus the selective detection of the target has been achieved. Herein, ssDNA plays dual roles in both improving the enzymatic activity and recognizing the target. This simple sensing design may be further employed to determinate other analytes (pathogens, metal ions and small molecules, etc.). # 2. Experimental section # 2.1. Reagents and chemicals Ti3C2Tx were purchased from Hangzhou Nanomall Co., Ltd. (Hangzhou, China). Tetramethylammonium hydroxide (TMAOH), dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), polyacrylic acid (PAA), tyrosine (Tyr), ascorbic acid (AA), NaCl, KCl, FeCl3 ⋅ 6H2O and o-phenylenediamine (OPD) were purchased from Shanghai Aladdin Biochemical Technology Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). Terephthalic acid (TA) and3,3 ′ ,5,5 ′ - tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) were bought from Sigma-Aldrich (St. Louis, MO, U. S.A.). Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2, 30wt%) was bought from Tianli Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (Tianjin, China). Ethyl alcohol (C2H5OH) and2, 2 ′ -azino-bis(3-ethylbenzothiazoline-6-sulfonic acid) (ABTS) were bought from Innochem Co., Ltd. (Beijing, China). Thrombin (TB) and Hemoglobin (Hb) were purchased from Sigma-Aldrich (St. Louis, MO, U. S.A.). They were dissolved in PBS buffer solution (pH7.0). The thrombin aptamer (TBA) was synthesized by Sangon Biotech Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). The base sequence is5 ′ -GGT TGG TGT GGT TGG-3 ′ . TBA was dissolved in Tris-HCl buffer solution (containing20mM Tris-HCl, 100mM NaCl, 5.0mM MgCl2 ⋅ 6H2O, 5.0mM KCl, 1.0mM CaCl2 ⋅ 2H2O, pH7.4). The human serum samples were provided by a local hospital and diluted with Tris -HCl buffer solution. All reactions were prepared with HAc-NaAc buffer (pH4.0, 200mM). # 2.2. Instruments Ultraviolet -visible (UV -vis) absorption spectra were carried out on an Agilent Cary8454ultraviolet visible spectrophotometer (Agilent, U. S.A.). Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR) was measured on a Tensor-27spectrometer (Bruker, Germany). Scanning Electron Microscope (SEM) images were taken with merlin compact (Zeiss, Germany). Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images were obtained with JEM2100 (Japan). X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) analysis of Ti3C2NSs nanomaterials was conducted on Escalab250Xi (Thermo, U.S.A.). The Zeta-potential was recorded by using a Zetasizer Nano ZS90 (Malvern, U.K.). M. Li et al. Fig. 1. (a) TEM image of Ti3C2NSs; (b) FTIR spectra of Ti3C2NSs; (c, d) XPS data for C1s and Ti2p of Ti3C2NSs, respectively. # 2.3. Synthesis of Ti3C2NSs Ti3C2NSs were synthesized using a method of intercalated micromolecule. First, a total of1.000g of Ti3C2Tx powders was dispersed in10mL of TMAOH (25wt% in water) under magnetic stirring at room temperature for24h. The intercalated Ti3C2NSs were collected by centrifugation and washed several times. Secondly, the product was redispersed into50mL of ultrapure water under bath sonication in N2atmosphere for60min. Eventually, the product was centrifugated at4000rpm for1h to obtain the supernatant and then disposed by freezedried samples for future usage [36,37]. # 2.4. Peroxidase-like activity and kinetic studies The peroxidase-like activity of Ti3C2NSs was demonstrated by using5.0 μ L of Ti3C2NSs (1.0mg/mL) in975 μ L HAc-NaAc buffer solution (pH4.0), followed by adding10 μ L of OPD solution (100mM) and10 μ L H2O2 (1.0M). Subsequently, the mixed solution was incubated at40 ◦ C for20min. Finally, the absorbance values were measured by using UV -vis absorption spectrophotometer at450nm. The peroxidase-like activity of Ti3C2@ssDNA was determined by a similar approach. 10 μ L different concentrations of ssDNA solution was added5.0 μ L of Ti3C2NSs (1.0mg/mL) containing965 μ L HAc-NaAc buffer solution (pH4.0), keeping for20min at room temperature, followed by adding10 μ L of OPD solution (100mM) and10 μ L H2O2 (1.0M). Subsequently, the mixed solution was incubated for20min at40 ◦ C. Eventually, the absorbance values were obtained at450nm. The kinetic studies were carried out by catalyzing the oxidation of the substrate of OPD in the presence of H2O2. All the reactions were performed in HAc-NaAc buffer solution, and the characteristic absorbance value was measured at450nm by using Ultraviolet -visible spectrometer. The steady-state kinetics of the peroxidase-mimicking activity of Ti3C2NSs was measured by changing the concentration of one substrate (OPD or H2O2), while keeping the other one fixed. Moreover, the Michaelis constants ( Km ) and maximum initial velocity ( Vmax) were obtained by using a Lineweaver-Burk plot. Particularly, Km represents the affinity of enzyme for the substrate, which is equivalent to the substrate concentration at which the conversion rate is half of Vmax. # 2.5. Target detection Briefly, 5.0 μ L of Ti3C2NSs (1.0mg/mL) and10 μ L of ssDNA (15 μ M) were added into955 μ L HAc-NaAc buffer solution (pH4.0). The solution was kept20min at room temperature. Then, 10 μ L different concentrations of target molecules were mixed with the above solution and kept for30min, successively. Finally, 10 μ L of OPD solution (120mM) and10 μ L H2O2 (2.5M) were added and kept at40 ◦ C for20min. The absorbance value at450nm was measured. # 3. Results and discussion # 3.1. Formation of Ti3C2nanomaterials The morphology and structure of Ti3C2nanomaterials were characterized by SEM, TEM, FTIR and XPS. After intercalation by organic base molecule (TMAOH) and delamination, large lateral-sized Ti3C2NSs were obtained with a sheet-like structure seen from Fig. 1a [32,34,35,38]. Fig. S1clearly shows Ti3C2nanomaterials with a layered structure. The M. Li et al. Fig. 2. (a) UV -Vis absorption spectra of different catalytic system. Black: 5.0 μ g/mL Ti3C2NSs + 1.0mM OPD; red: 1.0mM OPD + 10mM H2O2; blue: 5.0 μ g/mL Ti3C2NSs + 1.0mM OPD + 10mM H2O2under the condition of pH4.0HAc-NaAc buffer, 20min and40 ◦ C. (b) UV -Vis absorption values of Ti3C2NSs and Ti3C2@ssDNA in the presence of OPD and H2O2. (c) The colorimetric catalysis performances of Ti3C2@ssDNA depending on ssDNA concentrations. (d) The colorimetric catalysis performances of Ti3C2@ssDNA depending on different pH values. (b), (c), (d) under the condition of5.0 μ g/mL Ti3C2NSs, 150nM ssDNA, 1.0mM OPD10mM H2O2, pH4.0HAc-NaAc buffer, 20min and40 ◦ C. Error bars indicated standard deviations across three repetitive assays.. surface functional groups were characterized by FTIR spectroscopy. Fig. 1b shows the FTIR spectrum of Ti3C2NSs. The characteristic peaks mainly correspond to the stretching vibrations of -OH at around3015cm GLYPH<0> 1 , O -H of H2O at around1357cm GLYPH<0> 1and C -F at around948cm GLYPH<0> 1 . In particular, the peak at around646cm GLYPH<0> 1accounts for the vibration of the Ti -O bond [35,39]. The XPS survey scan spectrum of the Ti3C2NSs shows that the material was mainly composed of Ti, C, O and F. For the C1s spectrum (Fig. 1c), the peaks at286.2, 284.8and281.1eV correspond to C -O, C -C and C -Ti bonds, respectively [40]. In the case of the Ti2p peaks (Fig. 1d), the binding energy peaks located at454.8and460.7eV attributing to Ti -C bond and the binding energy peaks located at458.1and463.5eV corresponding to the Ti -O bond [41]. # 3.2. Investigation of peroxidase-like activity of Ti3C2NSs and Ti3C2@ssDNA The peroxidase mimicking activity of Ti3C2NSs was investigated by catalyzing the oxidation of the substrate of OPD in the presence of H2O2, with the absorbance recorded at450nm by using a UV -vis spectrophotometer. When Ti3C2NSs or H2O2coexisted with OPD for20min, the color of the solution is almost unchanged, consistent with weak absorbance at450nm (Fig. 2a). While Ti3C2NSs mixed with both OPD and H2O2, the absorbance at450nm increased greatly (Fig. 2a), indicating the formation of OPD oxidation products and the occurrence of the reaction catalyzed by Ti3C2NSs. This reveals Ti3C2NSs possess obvious peroxidase-like activity. We also studied the peroxidase-like activity of un-delaminated Ti3C2nanomaterials and found they have weak catalytic activity (Fig. S2). The high peroxidase-like activity of delaminated Ti3C2NSs may be due to the larger specific surface area and more exposed active sites than the layered structures (Table S1). The strong interaction between Ti3C2NSs and ssDNA has been confirmed [30]. Therefore, ssDNA can be used to modify Ti3C2NSs to form Ti3C2@ssDNA nanocomplex. As expected, this nanocomplex exhibits improved catalytic activity for OPD oxidation by H2O2, with the absorbance at450nm about1.5times higher than that of bare Ti3C2NSs (Fig. 2b). The effect of the amounts of ssDNA on Ti3C2NSs was studied. From Fig. 2c, the absorbance at450nm increased gradually as increasing the concentration of ssDNA, and reached a plateau at150nM indicating the highest catalytic activity of Ti3C2NSs was motivated until150nM of ssDNA. Thus, 150nM ssDNA was employed in this work. The influence of pH on the catalytic activity was also studied. Similar to natural peroxidase, both Ti3C2@ssDNA and Ti3C2NSs exhibit the enzymatic activities closely related to pH, with the highest at pH4.0 (Fig. 2d). In addition, the effects of the concentrations of OPD and H2O2on the catalytic performances of Ti3C2@ssDNA and Ti3C2NSs were researched. As can be seen from Fig. S3, both Ti3C2@ssDNA nanocomplex and Ti3C2NSs present the highest catalytic activity with the concentrations of1.2mM OPD and25mM H2O2. M. Li et al.

Table1Comparison of the kinetic parameters of Ti3C2NSs and Ti3C2@ssDNA. ( Km is the Michaelis-Menten constant, Vmax is the maximal reaction velocity). The mechanism of the chromogenic substrate catalyzed by Ti3C2NSs with peroxidase-like activity was demonstrated by fluorescence spectrometry. Here, terephthalic acid (TA) was chosen as a fluorescent probe, because it can capture · OH to generate2-hydroxyterephthalic acid (TAOH), which is a fluorescent product with a maximum emission wavelength of440nm. As shown Fig. S3c, a very strong fluorescence signal appeared on440nm under the excitation of315nm wavelength in the presence of Ti3C2NSs and H2O2. However, the fluorescence signal can be ignored in the presence of individual Ti3C2NSs or H2O2. The phenomenon indicated that H2O2can be decomposed to produce a large amount of · OH in the presence of Ti3C2NSs. Combined with the previous reports, the catalytic mechanism might be that H2O2could interact with Ti3C2NSs to produce · OH and OPD was oxidized by · OH to form a yellow product OPDox. # 3.3. Studies on ssDNA accelerating the peroxidase-like activity of Ti3C2NSs To further understand the mechanism of the catalytic activity, the steady-state kinetics were studied. Fig. S4a, c and a,c showed typical Michaelis-Menten curves for both bare Ti3C2NSs and Ti3C2@ssDNA nanocomplex. These data were fitted by double reciprocal plots (Fig. S4b, d and S5b, d) to obtain the steady-state kinetic parameters according to the Lineweaver GLYPH<0> Burk equation. The corresponding apparent Km and Vmax of different substrates were listed in Table1. In general, lower Km represents stronger affinity of enzyme to the substrate [42,43]. As can be seen from Table1, both Ti3C2NSs and Ti3C2@ssDNA nanocomplex have high Km values with H2O2as the substrate, demonstrating high concentrations of H2O2needed to reach the maximal catalytic activities. For OPD substrate, the Km value of Ti3C2@ssDNA nanocomplex is lower than that of unmodified Ti3C2NSs, revealing the nanocomplex has higher affinity toward OPD. It also proves that the oligonucleotides absorbed on the surface of nanosheets can promote the affinity between the enzyme and the substrate. Moreover, the Vmax catalyzed by Ti3C2@ssDNA achieve2.05times for H2O2and1.37times for OPD compared with Ti3C2NSs. These results illustrate Ti3C2@ssDNA nanocomplex possesses higher peroxidase-like catalytic activity than unmodified Ti3C2NSs, and ssDNA could indeed accelerate the peroxidase activity of Ti3C2NSs. The dynamic parameters reveal the increased enzymatic activity of Ti3C2NSs mainly originated from the oligonucleotides promoting the affinity between the nanozyme and OPD substrate. To explore the interaction mechanism, we also investigated the zeta potentials of Ti3C2NSs before and after modified with ssDNA. As shown in Fig. 3a, both Ti3C2NSs and Ti3C2@ssDNA nanocomplex are negatively charged. The adsorption of ssDNA on Ti3C2NSs brings about the decrease of zeta-potential from GLYPH<0> 9.16mV to GLYPH<0> 20.23mV suggesting that Ti3C2@ssDNA is more negative. The substrate OPD with amino groups (pKa = 4.46) brings positively charged at pH4.0. Thereby the enhanced Fig. 3. (a) Zeta potential of Ti3C2NSs and Ti3C2@ssDNA at pH4.0HAc-NaAc buffer. (b) Top: chemical structure of OPD, ABTS and TMB. Bottom: photograph of catalytic oxidation by Ti3C2NSs in the presence or absence of ssDNA. (c) Comparison of peroxidase-like activity of Ti3C2NSs coated with different negatively charged polymers. Condition: 5.0 μ g/mL Ti3C2NSs, 150nM ssDNA, 150nM PAA, 1.2mM OPD, 25mM H2O2, pH4.0HAc-NaAc buffer, 20min and40 ◦ C. Error bars indicated standard deviations across three repetitive assays. M. Li et al. Fig. 4. (a) UV -Vis spectra of Ti3C2NSs (black), Ti3C2@ssDNA (red) and Ti3C2@ssDNA + TB (blue) in the presence of OPD and H2O2. Condition: 5.0 μ g/mL Ti3C2NSs, 100nM ssDNA, 1.0 × 10GLYPH<0> 8M TB, 1.0mM OPD, 10mM H2O2, pH4.0HAc-NaAc buffer, 20min and40 ◦ C. (b) UV -Vis absorption spectra in the presence different concentration of TB from1.0 × 10GLYPH<0> 11to1.0 × 10GLYPH<0> 8M (inset: the liner relationship between absorbance and concentration). Condition: 5.0 μ g/mL Ti3C2NSs, 150nM ssDNA, different concentrations of TB, 1.2mM OPD, 25mM H2O2, pH4.0HAc-NaAc buffer, 20min and40 ◦ C. Error bars indicated standard deviations across three repetitive assays. electrostatic forces between the nanozyme and the substrate OPD may be the factor for ssDNA boosting their affinity. In addition, we also contrasted the catalytic performances Ti3C2NSs and Ti3C2@ssDNA toward other substrate oxidation reactions by H2O2, including positively charged TMB and negatively charged ABTS. TMB exhibits similar enhancement effect on the peroxidase activity of Ti3C2NSs by ssDNA, while ABTS displays an inhibition effect as a result of the electrostatic repulsion of ssDNA on Ti3C2NSs (Fig. 3b). Different negatively charged polymer PAA was also decorated on Ti3C2NSs to explain interaction between OPD and the modifier on Ti3C2NSs. Form Fig. 3c, both PAA and ssDNA coated Ti3C2NSs present higher catalytic activity than bare Ti3C2NSs, and ssDNA is more effective than PAA. This is probably attributed to the π GLYPH<0> π stacking between the benzene ring structure of OPD and the nucleobases of ssDNA. # 3.4. Colorimetric detection of TB with Ti3C2@ssDNA The promotion effect of ssDNA on the peroxidase activity of Ti3C2NSs endows it as a promising candidate for various biosensing applications. When aptamers were utilized to decorate the Ti3C2NSs, these oligonucleotides not only can enhance the enzymatic activity but also recognize the target molecules. In presence of the target, the aptamer was desorbed from Ti3C2NSs in virtue of the specific binding with target, leading to the decrease of the catalytic activity. Based on this principle, a simple colorimetric method for TB was developed by regulating the peroxidase catalytic activity of Ti3C2NSs with TBA. As shown in Fig. 4a, the absorbance at450nm significantly increased compared with Ti3C2NSs. After TB was added into the Ti3C2@ssDNA-containing reaction system, the absorbance at450nm decreased. Under the optimal experimental condition, the absorbance and the TB concentrations displayed a good linear relationship from1.0 × 10GLYPH<0> 11to1.0 × 10GLYPH<0> 8M. The limit of detection (LOD) is1.0 × 10GLYPH<0> 11M. The linear equation between the absorbance change and TB concentration exhibits as Δ A = -0.09402lgCTB GLYPH<0> 0.38013, with the correlation coefficient (R2 ) is0.9938. Moreover, we compared our work with the literatures and a comparative table has been given in Table S2 [44 -47]. To demonstrate the specificity of proposed Ti3C2@ssDNA colorimetric sensing platform, the selectivity of the sensor was also examined. As shown in Fig. S6a, different substances including Hb, Try, AA, Na + , K + and Fe3 + (1.0 × 10GLYPH<0> 8M) were selected for the specificity test. It was

SamplesAdded (nM)Found (nM)Recovery (%)RSD (%)

Table2Determination results in human serum samples. found that they have little effect on the TB detection. Besides, the storage stability of Ti3C2@ssDNA was explored (Fig. S6b). As expected, no significant absorbance changes of the Ti3C2@ssDNA were observed, even they were stored up to4weeks, indicating the long-term stability of the catalytic sensing strategy. The practical feasibility of the sensing strategy based on Ti3C2@ssDNA was evaluated for determination of TB in healthy human serum samples by the standard addition method under the optimal conditions. Human blood serum samples were collected by a local hospital. Then, 50 μ L of human serum was added to5.0mL0.1M PBS (pH7.4) as pretreatment serum. Subsequently, different concentrations of TB were injected into the pretreatment serum samples to assess the recoveries. Good recoveries of98.1% to101.1% were obtained, with the relative standard deviations (RSD) of3.0% to4.7% in Table2, indicating that the sensing strategy can successfully detect TB in human serum. # 4. Conclusions In summary, delaminated Ti3C2NSs with large specific surface area and more exposed active sites possess intrinsic peroxidase-like activity. Compared to bare Ti3C2NSs, the oligonucleotides adsorbed on Ti3C2NSs can boost the catalytic activity towards positively charged substrate OPD oxidation with a lower Km value and a higher Vmax value. Based on the peroxidase-like activity of Ti3C2NSs combined with the specific recognition of aptamer, a simple label-free colorimetric sensing platform for selective detection of TB was constructed. This proposed sensor presents good selectivity and sensitivity with a wide linear range and low limit of detection. This work exploits the biological application of Ti3C2MXenes as a mimetic enzyme. More significantly, this catalysisbased determination strategy can be employed for the analysis of various biological species by immobilizing the corresponding aptamers. M.
